description:
You are an automation specialist who has been asked to standardise the release-directory layout for an in-house web application that lives under:

/home/user/app

Your goal is to (a) add a new release, (b) wire up every required symbolic link so that the application can always reach the most-recent release and its shared configuration, and (c) write a concise operation log file that build-scripts can later parse to confirm that the procedure completed successfully.

PRE-EXISTING FILE-SYSTEM STATE
1. Directories
   • /home/user/app  
   • /home/user/app/releases  
   • /home/user/app/shared  
   • /home/user/app/logs  

2. Releases that already exist  
   • /home/user/app/releases/v1.0.0  
   • /home/user/app/releases/v1.1.0  

3. Inside each existing release directory the ONLY file present is  
   • README.txt  

4. Shared configuration file that every release must reach through a symlink:  
   • /home/user/app/shared/config.yml  
     Current contents (2 lines, Unix line-endings, trailing newline present):  
       db: prod  
       cache: redis  

5. A “current” symbolic link does NOT exist yet.

OBJECTIVES
Step through the following numbered goals.  The order matters because your log will be judged sequentially.

1. Create a brand-new release directory exactly at:  
      /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0  

2. Inside that directory create a plain-text file named README.txt whose sole contents (one line, terminating newline) are:  
      Version 1.2.0  

3. Set up /home/user/app/current as a symbolic link that points to the newly created release directory.  
   • The link must be relative (for portability).  It should resolve, from /home/user/app, to releases/v1.2.0  

4. For every release directory that now exists (v1.0.0, v1.1.0 and the new v1.2.0) create or update a file-level symbolic link called config.yml so that, when viewed from inside each release directory, it resolves to:  
      ../../shared/config.yml  
   i.e. the link itself must be stored as a relative path; do not hard-code an absolute path.

5. Produce an operation log at:  
      /home/user/app/logs/symlink_setup.log  
   The file must be overwritten (not appended) by this task.  
   EXACT FORMAT — every line MUST follow this ABNF:  
      TIMESTAMP SP ACTION SP TARGET  
   
   where  
   • TIMESTAMP is an RFC-3339 / ISO-8601 instant in UTC, e.g. 2023-06-25T14:01:30Z  
   • ACTION is one of the literal tokens shown below  
   • TARGET is either an absolute path (for directories/files) or, for symlinks,  
     “LINK_PATH -> LINK_DESTINATION” (exactly one space either side of “->”).  

   You must write one line for each of the six actions listed here and in the same order.  
     1. CREATED_DIR      /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0  
     2. CREATED_FILE     /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0/README.txt  
     3. CREATED_SYMLINK  /home/user/app/current -> releases/v1.2.0  
     4. CREATED_SYMLINK  /home/user/app/releases/v1.0.0/config.yml -> ../../shared/config.yml  
     5. CREATED_SYMLINK  /home/user/app/releases/v1.1.0/config.yml -> ../../shared/config.yml  
     6. CREATED_SYMLINK  /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0/config.yml -> ../../shared/config.yml  

   • The ACTION column value must match exactly (all caps, underscores as shown).  
   • Lines must appear in the numeric order above.  
   • Each line ends with a Unix newline (LF).  
   • Do NOT write any extra lines or comments.

VERIFICATION CHECK-LIST (what automated tests will look for)
A. Directory / file existence & permissions (default 755 for directories, 644 for files are fine).  
B. /home/user/app/current must be a symbolic link and must resolve to releases/v1.2.0 (relative).  
C. Every release directory must contain a config.yml link resolving to ../../shared/config.yml (relative).  
D. The new README.txt must contain exactly “Version 1.2.0\n”.  
E. /home/user/app/logs/symlink_setup.log must exist, contain exactly six lines, and each line must match the described format and order.

You do NOT have root privileges, so stay inside /home/user.  All required parent directories already exist and are writable.  No external network access is required.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING SET-UP (already on disk before the agent starts):

Directories:
/home/user/app
/home/user/app/releases
/home/user/app/releases/v1.0.0
/home/user/app/releases/v1.1.0
/home/user/app/shared
/home/user/app/logs

File: /home/user/app/releases/v1.0.0/README.txt
Content: "Version 1.0.0\n"

File: /home/user/app/releases/v1.1.0/README.txt
Content: "Version 1.1.0\n"

File: /home/user/app/shared/config.yml
Content (with terminating LF):
"db: prod\ncache: redis\n"

Nothing else exists under /home/user/app before the task begins.

EXPECTED END STATE AFTER SUCCESSFUL EXECUTION:

1. Directory /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0 exists.

2. File /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0/README.txt exists with content:
   "Version 1.2.0\n"

3. Symlink /home/user/app/current exists.
   • It is a link (not a directory or file).
   • It is stored as a relative link: releases/v1.2.0
   • readlink -f /home/user/app/current resolves to /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0

4. In every release directory the following symlink exists:
   /home/user/app/releases/vX.X.X/config.yml  (three copies)
   • Each symlink is relative and equals ../../shared/config.yml
   • All three resolve to /home/user/app/shared/config.yml when followed.

5. Log file /home/user/app/logs/symlink_setup.log exists.
   • Exactly six lines.
   • Each line ends with LF.
   • Lines are in this order and match this regular expression (POSIX-extended):
     ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z (CREATED_DIR|CREATED_FILE|CREATED_SYMLINK) .+$
   • The ACTION/target part of each line is, in order:
       1. CREATED_DIR /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0
       2. CREATED_FILE /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0/README.txt
       3. CREATED_SYMLINK /home/user/app/current -> releases/v1.2.0
       4. CREATED_SYMLINK /home/user/app/releases/v1.0.0/config.yml -> ../../shared/config.yml
       5. CREATED_SYMLINK /home/user/app/releases/v1.1.0/config.yml -> ../../shared/config.yml
       6. CREATED_SYMLINK /home/user/app/releases/v1.2.0/config.yml -> ../../shared/config.yml

Permissions do not need special checking; default umask values are acceptable.
